title: DaemonSet with Node Selector
description:
Restrict a DaemonSet to nodes labeled `role=worker` and observe it automatically scheduling Pods as node labels change.

**Focus**: Using `nodeSelector` to target a subset of nodes, showing that label changes on nodes — not DaemonSet edits — control Pod placement.

## Common Pitfalls
- If no nodes match the `nodeSelector`, the DaemonSet creates 0 pods silently — always verify with `kubectl get ds`.
- After labeling a node, the new Pod appears within seconds without restarting the DaemonSet.
- Removing the label from a node immediately terminates its DaemonSet Pod.
steps: Label your worker node(s) with role=worker: kubectl label node <node-name> role=worker
Create a DaemonSet named 'worker-agent' using busybox:1.36 with nodeSelector role=worker
Verify pods are running only on labeled nodes: kubectl get pods -l app=worker-agent -o wide
Check that the control-plane node (if present) has no worker-agent pod
Label an additional node with role=worker (or verify the DaemonSet DESIRED count matches labeled node count)
Run kubectl get ds worker-agent to confirm DESIRED matches number of role=worker nodes
hints: kubectl get nodes --show-labels helps you see current node labels
kubectl label node <name> role=worker adds the label to a specific node
nodeSelector sits inside spec.template.spec, not at the DaemonSet spec level
If DESIRED is 0, no nodes match your nodeSelector — check labels with kubectl get nodes --show-labels
kubectl get pods -o wide shows the NODE column to confirm placement

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: worker-agent
  labels:
    app: worker-agent
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: worker-agent
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: worker-agent
    spec:
      nodeSelector:
        # TODO: Only schedule on nodes labeled role=worker
      containers:
      - name: agent
        image: busybox:1.36
        command: ['sh', '-c', 'while true; do echo "Worker agent running"; sleep 60; done']
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "10m"
            memory: "16Mi"

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: worker-agent
  labels:
    app: worker-agent
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: worker-agent
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: worker-agent
    spec:
      nodeSelector:
        role: worker
      containers:
      - name: agent
        image: busybox:1.36
        command: ['sh', '-c', 'while true; do echo "Worker agent running"; sleep 60; done']
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "10m"
            memory: "16Mi"
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Label a worker node before applying
WORKER=$(kubectl get nodes --no-headers | grep -v control-plane | head -1 | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -z "$WORKER" ]; then
  # Single-node cluster — label the only node
  WORKER=$(kubectl get nodes --no-headers | head -1 | awk '{print $1}')
fi
kubectl label node "$WORKER" role=worker --overwrite
echo "✓ Labeled node $WORKER with role=worker"

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for DaemonSet
echo "Waiting for DaemonSet worker-agent..."
timeout=90
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  desired=$(kubectl get ds worker-agent -o jsonpath='{.status.desiredNumberScheduled}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  ready=$(kubectl get ds worker-agent -o jsonpath='{.status.numberReady}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  if [ "$desired" -gt 0 ] && [ "$ready" = "$desired" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5; ((elapsed+=5))
done

# Verify nodeSelector is set correctly
ns_role=$(kubectl get ds worker-agent -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.nodeSelector.role}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$ns_role" = "worker" ]; then
  echo "✓ nodeSelector role=worker is configured"
else
  echo "✗ nodeSelector role is '$ns_role', expected 'worker'"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify pods are only on labeled nodes
pods=$(kubectl get pods -l app=worker-agent -o wide --no-headers 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ -z "$pods" ]; then
  echo "✗ No worker-agent pods found"
  exit 1
fi

while IFS= read -r line; do
  pod_node=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $7}')
  node_label=$(kubectl get node "$pod_node" --show-labels 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'role=worker' || true)
  if [ "$node_label" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "✓ Pod on node $pod_node (has role=worker label)"
  else
    echo "✗ Pod found on node $pod_node which lacks role=worker label"
    exit 1
  fi
done <<< "$pods"

desired=$(kubectl get ds worker-agent -o jsonpath='{.status.desiredNumberScheduled}')
ready=$(kubectl get ds worker-agent -o jsonpath='{.status.numberReady}')
echo "✓ DaemonSet worker-agent: $ready/$desired ready"

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
